result:
1	---
     2	name: qjudge-exam-grading-sop
     3	description: Open-ended 題目 AI 批改 SOP(YAML)。
     4	---
     5	
     6	```yaml
     7	sop:
     8	  purpose: "Open-ended AI 批改:訂 rubric + seed grade.csv → 一題一題評 → 寫回。"
     9	
    10	  artifacts:
    11	    rubric.md:
    12	      writer: artifact_write
    13	      content_type: text/markdown
    14	
    15	    grade.csv:
    16	      seeder:   artifact_csv_from_json   # Stage 2 建檔
    17	      searcher: artifact_csv_search       # Stage 3/5 狀態檢查(不帶 rows)
    18	      loader:   artifact_csv_to_json      # Stage 3/5 取批次 payload
    19	      patcher:  artifact_csv_patch        # Stage 3/5 寫回部分變動
    20	      columns: [index, exam_answer_id, username, answer_text, original_score, original_feedback, score, reason, synced]
    21	      key_column: exam_answer_id
    22	
    23	  note: >
    24	    artifact 工具的 step 參數為 optional，正常流程省略即可；
    25	    工具會自動依 filename 定位唯一 artifact。僅在同 session 出現同名檔案時才需帶 step 消歧義。
    26	
    27	  stages:
    28	    - id: 1_target
    29	      out: context [contest_id, grading_question_id]
    30	      on_missing: 逐項問 user。
    31	
    32	    - id: 2_seed
    33	      steps:
    34	        - |
    35	          # 1. 產 rubric.md
    36	          artifact_write(
    37	            filename="rubric.md",
    38	            content=<markdown 評分標準>,
    39	            content_type="text/markdown",
    40	          )
    41	        - |
    42	          # 2. 抓答案
    43	          response = qjudge_grading(
    44	            action="list_answers",
    45	            contest_id=<X>, question_id=<Y>,
    46	            projection="grading",
    47	          )
    48	          # response 形狀: {"count": N, "items": [{exam_answer_id, username, answer_text, original_score, original_feedback}, ...]}
    49	        - |
    50	          # 3. seed grade.csv（records 帶 index + 5 欄，defaults 補 score/reason/synced = 9 欄）
    51	          artifact_csv_from_json(
    52	            filename="grade.csv",
    53	            records=response["items"],
    54	            defaults={"score": "", "reason": "", "synced": ""},
    55	          )
    56	        - |
    57	          # 4. 宣告批次計畫（每 20 筆一個 todo）
    58	          write_todos(todos=[
    59	            {"content": "批改 index 1-20 依 rubric.md",  "status": "pending"},
    60	            {"content": "批改 index 21-40 依 rubric.md", "status": "pending"},
    61	            ...
    62	          ])
    63	      out: [rubric.md, grade.csv (score/reason/synced 都空), todo list]
    64	
    65	    - id: 3_grade_batch_of_20
    66	      rules:
    67	        - 一題一題依 rubric.md 決定 score 與 reason，禁止寫腳本或機械批打。
    68	        - 禁止 artifact_read + offset/limit 手動找未評列；永遠用 artifact_csv_search / artifact_csv_to_json。
    69	        - reason 政策（省 output token）：
    70	            - 預設：滿分（rubric 最高分） → reason 留空；非滿分 → 必填 reason。
    71	            - 例外：滿分但有值得給的改進建議 → 可填。
    72	            - 覆寫：若 user 在 Stage 1 明說「每題都要寫 reason / 都要評語」 → 一律填。
    73	      loop:
    74	        - |
    75	          # 1. 看還剩幾筆沒評（便宜、不拉 rows）
    76	          status = artifact_csv_search(
    77	            filename="grade.csv",
    78	            where={"score": ""},
    79	          )
    80	          # status = {"matched": M, "total_rows": N}
    81	          # matched == 0 → 進 4_writeback_confirm
    82	        - |
    83	          # 2. 取本批 20 筆 + 必要兩欄（其他欄/列不載入，省 token）
    84	          batch = artifact_csv_to_json(
    85	            filename="grade.csv",
    86	            columns=["exam_answer_id", "answer_text"],
    87	            where={"score": ""},
    88	            limit=20,
    89	          )
    90	          # batch["records"] = [{exam_answer_id, answer_text}, ...]
    91	        - |
    92	          # 3. 對每一筆獨立思考後決定 score 與 reason（依 reason 政策）
    93	        - |
    94	          # 4. 寫回本批變動
    95	          artifact_csv_patch(
    96	            filename="grade.csv",
    97	            key_column="exam_answer_id",
    98	            updates=[
    99	              {"exam_answer_id": ..., "score": "...", "reason": "..."},
   100	              ... (本批 20 筆)
   101	            ],
   102	          )
   103	        - |
   104	          # 5. 更新 todo: 本批 completed，下一批 pending → in_progress
   105	          write_todos(todos=[...])
   106	        - status.matched > 0 → 下一輪；== 0 → 進 4_writeback_confirm
   107	
   108	    - id: 4_writeback_confirm
   109	      halt: true
   110	      precondition: |
   111	        artifact_csv_search(filename="grade.csv", where={"score": ""}) 回 matched == 0
   112	        （仍有空值 → 回 3_grade_batch_of_20 補完再進這裡）
   113	      branches:
   114	        contains_writeback_keyword: goto 5_writeback
   115	        other:                      ask_again (要「寫回」)
   116	      first_entry_message: |
   117	        全部 <N> 筆已評完（右側 panel 看 grade.csv）。
   118	        - 確認寫回 → 回「確認寫回」
   119	        - 取消 → 回「取消」
   120	
   121	    - id: 5_writeback
   122	      rules:
   123	        - 禁止手動解析 CSV 或寫 Python。search 看狀態 → to_json 組 payload → batch_grade → patch 標 synced。
   124	        - 每批最多 20 筆（跟 Stage 3 批次對齊）。
   125	        - 批次計畫前/每批完成後必 write_todos（跟 Stage 3 同規則）。
   126	      loop:
   127	        - |
   128	          # 1. 看還剩幾筆沒 sync
   129	          status = artifact_csv_search(
   130	            filename="grade.csv",
   131	            where={"synced": ""},
   132	          )
   133	          # matched == 0 → 跳出 loop 進 report
   134	        - |
   135	          # 2. 取這批 MCP payload（三欄）
   136	          batch = artifact_csv_to_json(
   137	            filename="grade.csv",
   138	            columns=["exam_answer_id", "score", "reason"],
   139	            where={"synced": ""},
   140	            limit=20,
   141	          )
   142	        - |
   143	          # 3. 整批寫回
   144	          qjudge_grading(action="batch_grade", grades=batch["records"])
   145	        - |
   146	          # 4. 成功的列標 synced="yes"；失敗的留空，等下輪重試或進 report
   147	          artifact_csv_patch(
   148	            filename="grade.csv",
   149	            key_column="exam_answer_id",
   150	            updates=[{"exam_answer_id": ..., "synced": "yes"}, ...],  # 只放成功的
   151	          )
   152	        - |
   153	          # 5. 更新 todo
   154	          write_todos(todos=[...])
   155	      report: 寫回 X 筆成功、Y 筆失敗（失敗列 exam_answer_id + 錯誤）。
   156	      idempotency: 同一 exam_answer_id 重複視為 no-op；synced=="yes" 的 row 下輪會自動被 where filter 跳過。
   157	
   158	  stage_resolution:
   159	    primary: artifact_list + artifact_csv_search
   160	    secondary: last_assistant_message
   161	    fallback: ask_user
   162	    map:
   163	      - {artifacts: [],                                    candidates: [1_target, 2_seed]}
   164	      - {artifacts: [rubric.md, grade.csv], score_empty>0: stage: 3_grade_batch_of_20}
   165	      - {artifacts: [rubric.md, grade.csv], score_empty=0: candidates: [4_writeback_confirm, 5_writeback]}
   166	
   167	  hard_rules:
   168	    - MCP 解析 / CSV 組裝必須在同一 turn 內自己做，禁 delegate task/subagent。
   169	    - 評分時一題一題思考並填 score，禁止寫腳本或機械批打。
   170	    - 任何批次操作「前」都要 write_todos 宣告批次計畫；「每批完成後」立刻 write_todos 把該批標 completed；todo 未更新 = 不可進下一批。
   171	    - 禁止用 artifact_read + offset/limit 找未評/未 sync 列；永遠用 artifact_csv_search（狀態）或 artifact_csv_to_json（取 rows）。
   172	    - 寫回前 user 訊息必須含「寫回」兩字。
   173	```

分批批改空白 score 的作答並立即寫回 grade.csv
確認 grade.csv 已無空白 score，結束批改

input_data.content:
# 批改準則：Demand Paging

- 題目滿分：2 分
- 評分重點：
  1. 需說明 demand paging 的概念：頁面只在需要時才載入記憶體，通常是在發生 page fault 時載入，不是程式一開始全載入。
  2. 需說明常用原因：
     - 減少初始載入時間
     - 節省實體記憶體，避免載入未使用頁面
     - 可支援超過實體記憶體大小的程式

- 配分建議：
  - 2 分：概念與用途/優點都說清楚，語意正確。
  - 1 分：只答對其中一部分，或描述大致正確但不完整。
  - 0 分：明顯錯誤、空白、或與 demand paging 無關。

- 可接受表述：
  - 「需要時才載入頁面」、「page fault 後才把頁面載入」
  - 「減少 memory usage」、「降低 startup time」、「可跑大於 RAM 的程式」

- 主要扣分情況：
  - 把 demand paging 說成預先全部載入
  - 只談虛擬記憶體但沒提 demand/on-demand 的核心概念
  - 只談一般 paging，未提需要時載入

input_data.content:
# 批改準則：Demand Paging

- 題目滿分：2 分
- 評分重點：
  1. 需說明 demand paging 的概念：頁面只在需要時才載入記憶體，通常是在發生 page fault 時載入，不是程式一開始全載入。
  2. 需說明常用原因：
     - 減少初始載入時間
     - 節省實體記憶體，避免載入未使用頁面
     - 可支援超過實體記憶體大小的程式

- 配分建議：
  - 2 分：概念與用途/優點都說清楚，語意正確。
  - 1 分：只答對其中一部分，或描述大致正確但不完整。
  - 0 分：明顯錯誤、空白、或與 demand paging 無關。

- 可接受表述：
  - 「需要時才載入頁面」、「page fault 後才把頁面載入」
  - 「減少 memory usage」、「降低 startup time」、「可跑大於 RAM 的程式」

- 主要扣分情況：
  - 把 demand paging 說成預先全部載入
  - 只談虛擬記憶體但沒提 demand/on-demand 的核心概念
  - 只談一般 paging，未提需要時載入

{"count": 20, "total_matched": 114, "records": [{"exam_answer_id": "1831", "answer_text": "把memory切成page 的大小去分配；這樣就不用受限於連續分配的問題"}, {"exam_answer_id": "1880", "answer_text": "Demand paging is a technique that only swap the pages when it's necessary. It is commonly used because it saves a lot of resource since it minimize the number of swapping."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2153", "answer_text": "透過valid bit檢查access的page是否在memory，如不在從secondory device (disk)中載入到memory，memory access比直接從disk讀資料更快，只載入需要的page到memory可節省memory用量"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5037", "answer_text": "不用將整個process所需的page一次輸入到memory中，只需輸入目前所需的apge即可，這樣可以有更多的空間做到同時有許多process在執行"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2160", "answer_text": "demand paging是在有需要的時候（process要reference這個page），才把他從second storage的swapping area交換出來，放到memory。
使用demand paging可以讓process的logical address規模超過physical address，讓更多process可以同時使用memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2152", "answer_text": "在需要頁面時才將他載入記憶體，而非一開始就將 process 所需的所有頁面都一次載入。
此作法的好處是使得 process 的啟動速度快；另外也可以讓 process 所需的所有頁面大小大於 memory 的空間，因為可以在工作的途中再替換成需要的頁面。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6029", "answer_text": "1. 透過一個 valid/invalid 去看檢查該 page 是否在 memory 裡
2. More efficient"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5679", "answer_text": "用valid/invalid bit(1/0)來表示那個page的資料是不是有用的，0表示沒用或是不在memory中，方便選擇page去做替換"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2254", "answer_text": "separate a process into different page, only load the page which is needed to RAM, so that RAM can have many processes with part of them, it commonly used because it can let process save more time to swap the whole process in and out."}, {"exam_answer_id": "1753", "answer_text": "Demand paging means we swap in the frames only when it is requested.
The virtual memory use a valid bit to show whether the page is valid or not.
It can make good use of the page table, since the pages usually more than frames.
And save spaces since some frames are not often required. Only swap in when we need it.
"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2058", "answer_text": "q1 : demand paging : The system load in the page when we need it.
q2 : because we have limit physical memory space, use demand paging can load the page only when we need it, save the memory usage, and more efficient. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "2347", "answer_text": "page與frame是分開的，page比frame還要多，因此系統會把用不到的page swap out到storage，在遭遇page fault的時候再swap in，能夠大幅減少physical frame的需求，減少page swap並增加效率"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6046", "answer_text": "When a process starts instead of loading the complete program into the memory, only the required parts to continue execution are loaded to the memory."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2004", "answer_text": "By using the concept of virtual paging, it achieves that(Why common use?) no limitation of the contiguous physical address and it's easy to check if the needs is on the memory by viewing the page table; if the needs is not on the memory, then do "swap in" from the disk and record it on demand page. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "2142", "answer_text": "in virtual memory system, process won't get physical memory for its all logical memory address, only ask for what it need. If a process need more memory, it will demand paging. Use virtual memory system let our computer run more processes even all needed memory of these processes more then physical memory."}, {"exam_answer_id": "1897", "answer_text": "Demand paging is when a process needs a page from its logical address space that is not currently loaded in the physical address space to be loaded in one of the frames. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "6488", "answer_text": "Demand paging is a concept when a process try to access to a data. Instead of accessing to the data thanks to the physical address (seen by the memory unit), it uses the logical address to simplify the memory management by the user, to secure the memory between the different users and by abstraction to allow more logical memory for a process than the actual physical memory. Therefore, when a process try to access to a data, it ask the MMU to translate the logical address to the physical address to get from a page, a frame."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2665", "answer_text": "讓執行中的 process 可以不用一次性載入所有需要的 memory frame / page，在有需要用到特定資料實在載入特定的 memory blocks。常被使用的原因有實作簡單，以及這樣的做法可以使比較大的 process 使用相對小的 memory blocks，從而增加整體運行的所需記憶體上限，減少 memory and disk 之間的資訊交換次數，增加效率，吞吐量等等。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "1933", "answer_text": "demand pageing 是要使用時才把這個page弄到memory裡，他這樣可以讓virtual momory比physcial memory大"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2082", "answer_text": "Demand paging is that bring a page into memory when it is needed.
It commonly used because it can reduce memory size."}]}

2. so it can have a large virtual memory with small physical memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6032", "answer_text": "because the data is too much, it can be reduce the memory space by demand paging."}, {"exam_answer_id": "1916", "answer_text": "virtual memory implementation是指logical address space與physical memory 的分離。當要進行execution時，只有部分的logical address space要在physical memory裡面，代表logical address space可以遠大於physical address space,讓一個physical memory可以分配給多個process

demand paging則是以一個bit表示，valid bit代表在尋找的page在memory中， invalid bit代表不在memory中"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5552", "answer_text": "demand paging is a mechanism that logical address page to physical memory. it's commonly used because page sizes is usually larger than frame sizes, so it needs to dynamically allocate physical memory resources."}, {"exam_answer_id": "1952", "answer_text": "當收到需求的時候才從記憶體存取過來，這樣不會浪費記憶體空間"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5697", "answer_text": "用一個bit來表達目標的page有沒有在memory裡面，如果是valid那就是有，如果是invalid那就可能是一個不合法的reference、或著是不在memory裡面

它受人喜愛的原因是只需要一個bit就能表達上面說的這麼多重要的訊息，非常方便且不怎麼占空間"}, {"exam_answer_id": "1868", "answer_text": "Demand paging is where we apply info to indicate if it is in memory or not, it is commomly used because pulling data from back store is relatively slow, so we just use it from memory if it is already there."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2017", "answer_text": "透過invalid和valid來劃分是否有對應的page能用
efficient，而且不易出錯"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2127", "answer_text": "because physical memory has limited size and logical memory doesn't, so we have to  do demand paging with logical memory to ensure physical memory does not over its limit."}, {"exam_answer_id": "4745", "answer_text": "用一個 bit 來表示 page 在不在 memory 內，bit=1 表示 valid， bit=0表示 invalid。
因為穩定且可以減少查找時間，所以常常被使用"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5973", "answer_text": "因為透過demand paging，以valid和invalid標記是否在memory，當需要資源時，可以去看狀態是valid還是invaild，看是否已經在memory，若是invaild，若legal則透過swaping in 到memory，可以請求目前需要的空間配置就好，logical address space 可以大於physical address space。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "4173", "answer_text": "Demand page指的是讓電腦在運行process時，不用每用到一次資料就要去讀全部的physical memory. 而是透過demand page去紀錄這些資料具體的位置，電腦只需讀取這些page table就可以直接找到資料。可以節省大量讓電腦從頭到尾重讀一次physical momery的時間，大幅增加效率，因此很常被使用。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2561", "answer_text": "virtual memory implementation is a technique that separate the logical address space and physical memory. when executing a process, logical adress space only load the frames to physical memory needed by the program. Therefore, logical address space can have more address than physical memory, which increases main memory utilization and improve CPU utilization.

It is commonly used because we can have more space and can utilize more memory, which is quite efficient."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2006", "answer_text": "在 virtual memory implementation 使用 logical addresses，分成valid/invalid把位址或真實資料記錄在 page 中，在需要時才去memory中找invalid的資料，可以節省資源和搜尋時間。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2156", "answer_text": "1. valid bit表示page在memory裡, 不用去硬碟裡找, invalid bit出現時才需要去找
2. demand paging可以節省資源"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2610", "answer_text": "Demand paging means that only part of logical address space needs to be in physical memory, it is commonly used because it can reduce memory occupation."}, {"exam_answer_id": "6609", "answer_text": "It separates user logical memory from physical memory, allowing programs larger than physical memory to execute by keeping only parts of the memory. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "1805", "answer_text": "因為virtual memory可以separate logical memory form physical memory，所以當程式執行時只需要載入會用到的page進入physical memory，這樣可以允許logical address space大於physical address space。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2606", "answer_text": "指使用 valid/invalid bit來表示此page是否在memory中 若無則需要去dish swapping 回來"}]}

{"count": 20, "total_matched": 54, "records": [{"exam_answer_id": "2048", "answer_text": "1.當某一個process被需要使用的時候就把需要的page replace進去memory。
2.可以節省memory的空間。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6343", "answer_text": "我們只要將要執行的程式載入memory中就好,不用把整個程式都載進去,
這樣可以讓我們的系統執行大小比記憶體還大的程式"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6599", "answer_text": "Demand paging refers to swapping data between logical address and physical memory when needed. This saves time accessing memory. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "6535", "answer_text": "Demand paging is a common phenomenon in virtual memory, as a valid bit means the page requested is in the memory, while an invalid bit may indicate that the requested page is not in memory."}, {"exam_answer_id": "5082", "answer_text": "Demanding page uses a bits to record whether the page is valid. Because the process can get the demanding page directly if it is valid, and don't need to access memory for many times."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2236", "answer_text": "Virtual memory separate logical address space and physical address space. It allows only part of logical address space in memory when execution. The pages will load into memory only when it was needed. It's called demand paging, and it allows the logical address space can be larger than physical address space, so it's commonly used."}, {"exam_answer_id": "5141", "answer_text": "We only bring those we need frame into memory, and it allows us to execute many processes at the same time, which increases the throughput. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "2008", "answer_text": "Demand paging 是當某一page被referenced 時，才將此page 從disk swap in memory 。此一做法的最大好處為 ，process大小不再受限於physical memory size . "}, {"exam_answer_id": "1662", "answer_text": "because the memory size is not enough for some system and not every program are use all the time"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2184", "answer_text": "記憶體會管理 valid bit 和 invalid bit 去增加效率、把需要的記憶體的 page 在實際要用到的時候載入相鄰的 page，使得他可以更好的去利用 locality，因為大部分的應用程式用到的記憶體都在相鄰的部分，所以效率更高"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2027", "answer_text": "partially loading the process's page instead of loading all the pages.
it's commonly used since it can reduce the amount of loadinng pages while executing the process."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2041", "answer_text": "1.把pages標記是否為valid來方便在做swapping時確認 能不能用/有沒有被更換過
2.因為memory不是每個部分都是時時刻刻都在用的，把沒那麼常用的memory pages暫時擱置在後面(disk)可以讓memory使用時等幾乎等效於有更多位置的virtual memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "1857", "answer_text": "demand paging 的實作方式 是 process 使用的記憶空間 不會一開始全部都放進 memory ， 而是在需要時再換入 MEMORY 當中。 而他之所以廣泛使用是因為記憶體的空間有限且 process 具有 LOCALITY 如果把整個 PROCESS 全部放入反而會造成資源的浪費"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5862", "answer_text": "it can save time for memory transfering , only when a page is really needed and it is currently in the disk , then it will be read to the main memory . It is commonly used because it saves time to useless tranfer of data . "}, {"exam_answer_id": "2248", "answer_text": "Demand paging : 只有在該 page 需要被用到時才會被 load into physical memory.
It is commonly used because it could reduce memory 消耗量，並且可以使得 logical address space much larger than physical address. 可以執行比 physical memory 容量大的程式 "}, {"exam_answer_id": "1924", "answer_text": "當需要page的時候才載入到memory，可以節省memory空間"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6027", "answer_text": "Divide file to pages. When file is to be used, trans pages from disk to memory let memory look like has a big size.
When program has bigger size than memory, this technic can let program run in memory."}, {"exam_answer_id": "6294", "answer_text": "the physical memory may be small and not continuous for the process' need
virtual memory make process think it has a big continuous memory to use
and page is part of the process' virtual memory
process can demand paging to make the page be put in physical memory to use it
so there's no need to always keep all page in physical memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6437", "answer_text": "只有需要的page會被載入，減少冗於的空間分配，vitual memory相對於physical memory也可以大得多。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2583", "answer_text": "page只在被需要用到的時候寫入physical memory ,因為減少不必要的memory占用，不需要將所有東西一次存入physical memory，所以被廣泛使用"}]}

{"count": 20, "total_matched": 34, "records": [{"exam_answer_id": "2203", "answer_text": "將儲存空間paging成數個frame，需要的process只要記得對應frame的logical address，透過valid-bit判斷是否需要額外行動，因為不需要在記憶體找連續空間以及解決external fragment的問題而被經常使用"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2292", "answer_text": "in virtual memory, logical memory can be larger than physical memory, which means it can run a process that the size is larger than the physical memory, but it needs demand paging.
when a needed page is not in the physical memory, OS will take it from the backing store(which is in the disk).
it is commonly used because it has many advantage, such as memory sharing, dynamic loading, etc."}, {"exam_answer_id": "1888", "answer_text": "記憶體中為了運行一個程式中最少需要擁有的pages，並只會在需要用到時放入frame中，這個方法能夠最省記憶體空間，且使程式能夠進行不會崩潰。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2219", "answer_text": "if there is an invald reference,the os will bring the missing  page into memory;if the page is simply not in memory,the process will be abored."}, {"exam_answer_id": "6143", "answer_text": "it can efficiently know the address, without calling the memory which needs lots of time"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5022", "answer_text": "透過分離logical address 與physical memory來增加能夠運行的檔案大小。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2049", "answer_text": "當某一個page被referenced時（需要使用時），才將此page從disk去swap到memory，好處是可以不用先佔memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2833", "answer_text": "Demand paging means there exists a bit to control the page. If the bit is valid, it means the page is in the memory. If it is invalid, then it may be an illegal reference or the page is not in the memory. 
It is commonly used because it's easy to control, and the system does not need to load the whole data into the physical memory. Therefore, the CPU will think the logical memory has a large and clean space.  "}, {"exam_answer_id": "1955", "answer_text": "demand paging means to request new pages when needed, Instead of allocated bunch of pages at first, it's much more efficient.
"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2272", "answer_text": "用TLB查demand paging 在virtual memory實際用在哪個實體記憶體"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2696", "answer_text": "因為它具有擴展性，可以擴充更更多記憶體容量，因為被廣泛使用。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2223", "answer_text": "A valid bit means the page is in memory; an invalid bit may indicate either an illegal reference or the page is not in memory."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2303", "answer_text": "(a) The demand paging is a dynamic memory allocation technique where a process needs to access data from memory (hardware) but it is not in the page table. It sets a trap and wait the required data block retrieved from the main memory.
(b) The technique is commonly used because a process can start running without lots memory management time, which the "demand paging" helps easily manage the data to the process when needed."}, {"exam_answer_id": "2410", "answer_text": "這是一種將使用者邏輯位址空間與實體位址空間分離的技術，只要部份的邏輯位址空間存在於實體記憶體中，就可以執行程式，可以有效節省記憶體空間。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "3061", "answer_text": "以虛記憶體為需求分頁導向的概念，避免記憶體頁框"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6056", "answer_text": "在page table中標示valid代表page在memory中，invalid代表invalid reference或page不在memory中，invalid會直接miss掉，否則會把這個page mapping到memory中
把logical address space切成多個pages，physical memory切成多個frames，再用page table紀錄可以節省很多記憶體，不用把整塊logical address space放進physical memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2383", "answer_text": "it is used to avoid internal fragmentation(avoid it get a page larger then it need,the unused part can't be used by other either)"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6304", "answer_text": "可根據需求決定用多少分頁，不用利用實體記憶體只需藉由虛擬記憶體執行，提高效率。
a valid bit means the page is in memory,an invalid bit means the page is not in the memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "6314", "answer_text": "執行程式的時候，只需要部分virtual memory 在physical memory 中即可，因此virtual memory可以大於physical memory
可以讓physical memory被多個processor分享
可以讓messaging更efficient"}, {"exam_answer_id": "4699", "answer_text": "a valid bit that means the page is in memory, and an invalid bits means either an illegal reference or the page is not in memory.
it is commonly used because it increase the efficient of process executing, only the data needed will load to the memory."}]}

{"count": 14, "total_matched": 14, "records": [{"exam_answer_id": "6551", "answer_text": "用一個bit就可以知道這筆資料是不是在memory裡面"}, {"exam_answer_id": "1982", "answer_text": "moving data from backing store only when the process need it.

because it can decrease unnecessary data transfer between physical memory and backing store. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "5103", "answer_text": "It use a valid digit to describe the page is in RAM or not.
It allows the sum of the working set greater than the space of RAM in the concept of we just need to put the necessary page into RAM at that moment."}, {"exam_answer_id": "5228", "answer_text": "在表中valid bit表示page在記憶體中，當表中為invalid bit 時表示illegal reference或是page不在記憶體中。
"}, {"exam_answer_id": "1969", "answer_text": "demand paging 是當我們的程式需要用到那個page的是後才load到memory，這樣不用把所有的都load進memory,可以讓virtual memory的大小大於physical的memory能更有效的使用memory"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5896", "answer_text": "demand paging is that process can just load running part of process  into physical memory, so the process size(logical space) can larger then physical memory space.  
and it can swap data between physical memory and backing store, and use valid/invalid bit to decide swap flow. "}, {"exam_answer_id": "5233", "answer_text": "page table中，valid bit為1代表page在memory中，而為0則可能為illegal 或page不在memory中，需要再去次級memory 中找對應的data
page table中，使用valid bit可以更快判斷memory中是否有對應的page以提升效率"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2024", "answer_text": "demand paging是指當有需要使用某個page時才會將它load到memory，這樣做可以節省很多實體memory的空間，讓系統更有效率"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2591", "answer_text": "如果有needed page需要存放於memory，可以透過valid-invalid-bit檢查page是否在memory中。
因為這樣可以節省memory 的空間，避免overhead(不必要的page占用memory)"}, {"exam_answer_id": "5781", "answer_text": "demand page就是會有一個bit 如果他是valid bit 表示他在memory裡面如果是invalid bit 則表示他沒有在memory裡面需要去搬運或是是illegal reference。因為demanding page運用在paging table上能讓整個搜尋過程比較快速有效率"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2260", "answer_text": "demand paging 就是OS依據valid bit 來判斷虛擬記憶體是否有空位，常被用在動態分配記憶體(malloc)"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2227", "answer_text": "page在需要的時候才會載入，大部分都放在virtual memory，可以減少存放在physical memory的大小，讓更多process可以共用memory。並且透過valid bit來得知需要的page有沒有在memory裡。"}, {"exam_answer_id": "2550", "answer_text": "Demand paging is the technique that load the page only when a page is needed. It can help optimize memory utilization."}, {"exam_answer_id": "1920", "answer_text": "執行某process時需要用到該page才把page載入到memory中
避免消耗太多memory space"}]}
